name: Mission Dolores Park

permits.description: new fire sprinkler system installed throughout. (n) 4" fire service off of 8" main on 18th st by other. ref app#201307081348 bldg 1

4 inches sprinkler ground (plumbing fire water line) at the o&m building #1 at 598 dolores st., connecting to the 8" on 18th street. sprinklers under pa#201405014680- under ground approved conditional that sprinklers will match up.

like for like system replacement with new control panel, smoke det and pull station ats same location install radio comm new monitor modules to tie in e flow and tamper switch and relay modules to shutdown hvac system

summary_en: Mission Dolores Park appears in a 2008 Clean & Safe Neighborhood Parks bond renovation listing, Controller park-score reports for FY2008-09 and FY2009-10, and a 1975 Open Space fund report noting $70,000 for children's play apparatus. A long run of commission and committee minutes from the 1990s and 2000s centers on the recurring debate over a youth soccer field and playground improvements. The park is operated by the Recreation and Park Department (FY2025 budget line ~$250M), and a city contract with Race to Zero Waste for the 'Dolores Park Eco' project has paid about $294K.
